title: oxalis bartolomensis
intro: Oxalis bartolomensis, also known as the Mexican wood sorrel, is a species of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae). It has been used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of oxalic acid, which is believed to have antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The plant is used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, and is also believed to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Oxalis bartolomensis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine in Mexico and other parts of Latin America.
